title: Native Rust agent
scenario: Generate a native Rust agent when a skill is insufficient, using one core behavior implementation with HTTP/OpenAI-compatible, MCP, ACP, AG-UI, and A2A adapters only as needed.
termination: Stop only when the generated agent builds, launches, exposes each declared protocol through real consumers, shares one behavior core across adapters, has Docker proof, and passes skill-versus-agent justification review.

prompts.prerequisites: Verify stable job definition, protocol consumers, runtime choice, config/security model, Docker target, and whether a skill would be sufficient instead of an agent.
prompts.discovery: Read native-agent creator instructions, Axum/Rust patterns, protocol docs, OpenAI proxy case study, packaging conventions, and current repository templates.
prompts.feynman: Explain why this capability needs a native agent rather than a skill, and how one core behavior maps to protocol adapters without duplicate logic.
prompts.kbd_assess: Assess capability boundary, consumers, protocols, persistence, auth, configuration, observability, packaging, and operator deployment needs.
prompts.kbd_analyze: Analyze generator outputs, one-core/adapters architecture, Axum routes, MCP/ACP/AG-UI/A2A contracts, Dockerfile, and integration tests.
prompts.kbd_spec: Specify core traits, adapters, config, health/readiness, protocol consumers, Docker image, security defaults, and public-boundary tests.
prompts.kbd_plan: Plan capability classification first, then generator invocation, core implementation, adapters, protocol tests, Docker launch, critic, and retention.
prompts.research: Use official protocol documentation and local case-study source to confirm current adapter requirements; do not copy stale model catalogs or unsupported auth patterns.
prompts.implementation: Generate or implement the agent with one behavior core. Add only required adapters. Keep protocol glue thin and test each declared consumer.
prompts.public_boundary_verification: Build the agent, run it, exercise each declared protocol with a real consumer or smoke request, build the Docker image, and prove health/readiness.
prompts.independent_critic: Review whether a skill would have been enough, adapters duplicate logic, protocols are real, Docker proof exists, security defaults are safe, and the case-study lessons were applied correctly.
prompts.reflection_retention: Record generator gaps, protocol decisions, adapter lessons, packaging proof, and whether a new reusable skill/template is needed.
prompts.recovery: If a protocol adapter fails twice, stop and either narrow declared protocols or return to KBD analysis for that adapter contract.
prompts.stop_conditions: Stop for unclear job definition, no consumer, duplicated adapter logic, unsupported protocol claims, failed launch, missing Docker proof, or unjustified native-agent scope.
